attacks.name: Blazing Burst
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken. Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: V-Force
attacks.effect: If you have 4 or fewer Benched Pokémon, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

rules:
Search your deck for an Evolution Pokémon and an Energy card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

attacks.name: Gold Breaker
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 100 more damage.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dark Bite
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Power Press
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 80 more damage.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Gale Thrust
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Sonic Edge
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Blazing Burst
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken. Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Energy Gift
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Wondrous Cotton
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 50 damage for each Trainer card you find there.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Search your deck for an Evolution Pokémon and an Energy card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Each player shuffles their hand into their deck. Then, flip a coin. If heads, you draw 5 cards, and your opponent draws 3 cards. If tails, you draw 3 cards, and your opponent draws 5 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

attacks.name: Gold Breaker
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 100 more damage.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dark Bite
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Power Press
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra Energy attached (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 80 more damage.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Gale Thrust
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Sonic Edge
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Blazing Burst
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken. Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Energy Gift
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Wondrous Cotton
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 50 damage for each Trainer card you find there.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Odor Sleuth
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. Put a number of cards up to the number of heads from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Special Fang
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: It rescues people stranded by blizzards in the mountains. Its shaggy fur shields it from the cold.

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.effect: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

flavorText: It has black, cape-like fur that is very hard and decreases the amount of damage it receives.

attacks.name: Play Rough
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It faces strong opponents with great courage. But, when at a disadvantage in a fight, this intelligent Pokémon flees.

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Hyper Fang attack's base damage is 240.

attacks.name: Hyper Fang
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: When they see an enemy, their tails stand high, and they spit the seeds of berries stored in their cheek pouches.

attacks.name: Procurement
attacks.effect: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Using food stored in cheek pouches, they can keep watch for days. They use their tails to communicate with others.

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It warms its body by absorbing sunlight with its wings. When its body temperature falls, it can no longer move.

attacks.name: Bite Together
attacks.effect: If Durant is on your Bench, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: They attack in groups, covering themselves in steel armor to protect themselves from Heatmor.

attacks.name: Power Whip
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It fights by swinging around its three spiky feelers. A hit from these steel spikes can reduce a boulder to rubble.
When threatened, it attacks by shooting a barrage of spikes, which gives it a chance to escape by rolling away.

attacks.name: Double Hit
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: After it has eaten up all the food in its territory, it moves to another area. Its two heads do not get along.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It tends to bite everything, and it is not a picky eater. Approaching it carelessly is dangerous.
It makes a nest out of bones it finds. It grabs weakened prey in its talons and hauls it to its nest of bones.
Its wings are too tiny to allow it to fly. As the time approaches for it to evolve, it discards the bones it was wearing.

attacks.name: Mind Jack
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Foul Play
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

flavorText: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

attacks.name: Gunk Shot
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It clenches opponents with its left arm and finishes them off with foul-smelling poison gas belched from its mouth.
Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.

attacks.name: Ruffians Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Darkness Pokémon you have in play. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Groups of them beat up anything that enters their territory. Each can spit acidic liquid from its mouth.
Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling its skin up to its neck.

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.effect: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: These Pokémon vanish and appear unexpectedly. Many Trainers are drawn to their beautiful form and fur.

attacks.name: Invite Evil
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Darkness Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: They steal from people for fun, but their victims can't help but forgive them. Their deceptively cute act is perfect.

attacks.name: Retaliate
attacks.effect: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon came to the defense of Pokémon that had lost their homes in a war among humans.

attacks.name: Smash Uppercut
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It wields the fur on its arms like a whip. Its arm attacks come with such rapidity that they cannot even be seen.
In fights, they dominate with onslaughts of flowing, continuous attacks. With their sharp claws, they cut enemies.
They are intelligent and will cooperate to catch prey. From the ground, they use a running start to take flight.

attacks.name: Acrobatics
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Said to be an ancestor of bird Pokémon, they were unable to fly and moved about by hopping from one branch to another.

attacks.name: Rising Chop
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon isn't a Pokémon ex, this attack does nothing. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: The sound of Sawk punching boulders and trees can be heard all the way from the mountains where they train.

attacks.name: Vengeful Cannon
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on all of your Benched Fighting Pokémon.

flavorText: Compressing the energy from its internal core lets it fire off an attack capable of blowing away a mountain.

attacks.name: Smack Down
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has Fighting Resistance, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: When it overflows with power, the orange crystal on its body glows. It looks for underground water in caves.

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks if that damage is 40 or less.

flavorText: Its ear is hexagonal in shape. Compressed underground, its body is as hard as steel.

attacks.name: Oceanic Gloom
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

flavorText: With its thin, veil-like arms wrapped around the body of its opponent, it sinks to the ocean floor.

attacks.name: Fortunate Eye
attacks.effect: Look at the top 5 cards of your opponent's deck and put them back in any order.

flavorText: They use hypnosis to control people and Pokémon. Tales of Gothorita leading people astray are told in every corner.
Their ribbonlike feelers increase their psychic power. They are always staring at something.

attacks.name: Extended Damagriiigus
attacks.effect: Move all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Perplex
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It has been said that they swallow those who get too close and turn them into mummies. They like to eat gold nuggets.

attacks.name: Focused Wish
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Each of them carries a mask that used to be its face when it was human. Sometimes they look at it and cry.

attacks.name: Reflect
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 40 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Telekinesis
attacks.effect: This attack does 70 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: They never vary the route they fly, because their memories of guarding an ancient city remain steadfast.

attacks.name: Happy Return
attacks.effect: Put 1 of your Benched Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.

flavorText: It emits sound waves of various frequencies from its nose, including some powerful enough to destroy rocks.
Its habitat is dark forests and caves. It emits ultrasonic waves from its nose to learn about its surroundings.

attacks.name: Muddy Bolt
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Fighting Energy attached, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Its skin is very hard, so it is unhurt even if stepped on by sumo wrestlers. It smiles when transmitting electricity.

attacks.name: Discharge
attacks.effect: Discard all Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: When attacked, they create an electric barrier by spitting out many electrically charged threads.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Joltik that live in cities have learned a technique for sucking electricity from the outlets in houses.

attacks.name: Electrobullet
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.
Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use their flashing manes to communicate with one another.

attacks.name: Double Freeze
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage for each heads. If either of them is heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and attack their foes with violent blizzards.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Snowy mountains are this Pokémon's habitat. During an ancient ice age, they moved to southern areas.

attacks.name: Ice Edge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: The temperature of their breath is -58° F. They create snow crystals and make snow fall in the areas around them.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Swanna start to dance at dusk. The one dancing in the middle is the leader of the flock.

attacks.name: Firefighting
attacks.effect: Discard a Fire Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: These bird Pokémon are excellent divers. They swim around in the water eating their favorite food--peat moss.

attacks.name: Bared Fangs
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has no damage counters on it before this attack does damage, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Red and blue Basculin get along so poorly, they'll start fighting instantly. These Pokémon are very hostile.

attacks.name: Energized Slash
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 more damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: One swing of the sword incorporated in its armor can fell an opponent. A simple glare from one of them quiets everybody.

attacks.name: Energized Shell
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Strict training is how it learns its flowing double-scalchop technique.
It fights using the scalchop on its stomach. In response to an attack, it retaliates immediately by slashing.

attacks.name: Licking Catch
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 in any combination of Fire Pokémon and Basic Fire Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It breathes through a hole in its tail while it burns with an internal fire. Durant is its prey.

attacks.name: Incendiary Pillar
attacks.effect: If you have 10 or more Basic Fire Energy cards in your discard pile, this attack does 100 more damage.

attacks.name: Burn It All Up
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It absorbs a spirit, which it then burns. By waving the flames on its arms, it puts its foes into a hypnotic trance.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: This ominous Pokémon is feared. Through cities it wanders, searching for the spirits of the fallen.

attacks.name: Brighten and Burn
attacks.effect: Look at the top card of your deck. You may discard that card.

flavorText: Litwick shines a light that absorbs the life energy of people and Pokémon, which becomes the fuel that it burns.
It loves sweets because they become energy for the fire burning inside its body.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: When it is angered, the temperature of its head tuft reaches 600° F. It uses its tuft to roast berries.
It can throw a fire punch by setting its fists on fire with its fiery chin. It cares deeply about its friends.
When its internal fire flares up, its movements grow sharper and faster. When in trouble, it emits smoke.
It can deftly dodge its foe's attacks while shooting fireballs from its nose. It roasts berries before it eats them.

attacks.name: Giga Drain
attacks.effect: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Emerald Blade
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use attacks.

flavorText: This Pokémon fought humans in order to protect its friends. Legends about it continue to be passed down.

attacks.name: Acid Spray
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: When its body dries out, it weakens. So, to prevent dehydration, it wraps itself in many layers of thin membrane.
When attacked, it defends itself by closing the lid of its shell. It can spit a sticky, poisonous liquid.

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.effect: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

flavorText: They migrate according to the seasons. People can tell the season by looking at Sawsbuck's horns.
The color and scent of their fur changes to match the mountain grass. When they sense hostility, they hide in the grass.

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When attacked, it escapes by shooting cotton from its body. The cotton serves as a decoy to distract the attacker.

attacks.name: Healing Wrapping
attacks.effect: Heal 100 damage from each of your Basic Pokémon.

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves, using the cutters on its arms and sticky silk.
Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.
Leavanny dress it in clothes they made for it when it hatched. It hides its head in its hood while it is sleeping.

rules: If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of your turn.  As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides Colorless Energy.  If this card is attached to an Evolution Pokémon, it provides ColorlessColorlessColorless Energy instead.

Choose up to 2 Pokémon Tools attached to Pokémon (yours or your opponent's) and discard them.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Search your deck for an Evolution Pokémon and an Energy card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Each player shuffles their hand into their deck. Then, flip a coin. If heads, you draw 5 cards, and your opponent draws 3 cards. If tails, you draw 3 cards, and your opponent draws 5 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Put up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

If the Pokémon this card is attached to doesn't have a Rule Box, the attacks it uses do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon ex (before applying Weakness and Resistance). (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)

You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Play this card as if it were a 60-HP Basic Colorless Pokémon. This card can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat.  At any time during your turn, you may discard this card from play.

You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

attacks.name: Wrapped in Wind
attacks.effect: Attach a Basic Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hurricane
attacks.effect: Move a Basic Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.

attacks.name: Gold Breaker
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex, this attack does 100 more damage.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.effect: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Starry skies thousands of light-years away are visible in the space distorted by their intense psychic power.

attacks.name: Fortunate Eye
attacks.effect: Look at the top 5 cards of your opponent's deck and put them back in any order.

flavorText: Each of them carries a mask that used to be its face when it was human. Sometimes they look at it and cry.

attacks.name: Reflect
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 40 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Telekinesis
attacks.effect: This attack does 70 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: They never vary the route they fly, because their memories of guarding an ancient city remain steadfast.

attacks.name: Happy Return
attacks.effect: Put 1 of your Benched Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.

flavorText: It emits sound waves of various frequencies from its nose, including some powerful enough to destroy rocks.
Its habitat is dark forests and caves. It emits ultrasonic waves from its nose to learn about its surroundings.

attacks.name: Muddy Bolt
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Fighting Energy attached, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Its skin is very hard, so it is unhurt even if stepped on by sumo wrestlers. It smiles when transmitting electricity.

attacks.name: Discharge
attacks.effect: Discard all Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: When attacked, they create an electric barrier by spitting out many electrically charged threads.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Joltik that live in cities have learned a technique for sucking electricity from the outlets in houses.

attacks.name: Electrobullet
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.
Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use their flashing manes to communicate with one another.

attacks.name: Gale Thrust
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Sonic Edge
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Double Freeze
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage for each heads. If either of them is heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and attack their foes with violent blizzards.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Snowy mountains are this Pokémon's habitat. During an ancient ice age, they moved to southern areas.

attacks.name: Ice Edge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: The temperature of their breath is -58° F. They create snow crystals and make snow fall in the areas around them.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Swanna start to dance at dusk. The one dancing in the middle is the leader of the flock.

attacks.name: Firefighting
attacks.effect: Discard a Fire Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: These bird Pokémon are excellent divers. They swim around in the water eating their favorite food--peat moss.

attacks.name: Bared Fangs
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has no damage counters on it before this attack does damage, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Red and blue Basculin get along so poorly, they'll start fighting instantly. These Pokémon are very hostile.

attacks.name: Energized Slash
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 more damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: One swing of the sword incorporated in its armor can fell an opponent. A simple glare from one of them quiets everybody.

attacks.name: Energized Shell
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Strict training is how it learns its flowing double-scalchop technique.
It fights using the scalchop on its stomach. In response to an attack, it retaliates immediately by slashing.

attacks.name: Blazing Burst
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken. Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Licking Catch
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 in any combination of Fire Pokémon and Basic Fire Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It breathes through a hole in its tail while it burns with an internal fire. Durant is its prey.

attacks.name: Incendiary Pillar
attacks.effect: If you have 10 or more Basic Fire Energy cards in your discard pile, this attack does 100 more damage.

attacks.name: Burn It All Up
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It absorbs a spirit, which it then burns. By waving the flames on its arms, it puts its foes into a hypnotic trance.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: This ominous Pokémon is feared. Through cities it wanders, searching for the spirits of the fallen.

attacks.name: Brighten and Burn
attacks.effect: Look at the top card of your deck. You may discard that card.

flavorText: Litwick shines a light that absorbs the life energy of people and Pokémon, which becomes the fuel that it burns.
It loves sweets because they become energy for the fire burning inside its body.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: When it is angered, the temperature of its head tuft reaches 600° F. It uses its tuft to roast berries.
It can throw a fire punch by setting its fists on fire with its fiery chin. It cares deeply about its friends.
When its internal fire flares up, its movements grow sharper and faster. When in trouble, it emits smoke.
It can deftly dodge its foe's attacks while shooting fireballs from its nose. It roasts berries before it eats them.

attacks.name: Giga Drain
attacks.effect: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Emerald Blade
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use attacks.

flavorText: This Pokémon fought humans in order to protect its friends. Legends about it continue to be passed down.

attacks.name: Acid Spray
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: When its body dries out, it weakens. So, to prevent dehydration, it wraps itself in many layers of thin membrane.
When attacked, it defends itself by closing the lid of its shell. It can spit a sticky, poisonous liquid.

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.effect: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

flavorText: They migrate according to the seasons. People can tell the season by looking at Sawsbuck's horns.
The color and scent of their fur changes to match the mountain grass. When they sense hostility, they hide in the grass.

attacks.name: Energy Gift
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Wondrous Cotton
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 50 damage for each Trainer card you find there.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When attacked, it escapes by shooting cotton from its body. The cotton serves as a decoy to distract the attacker.

attacks.name: Healing Wrapping
attacks.effect: Heal 100 damage from each of your Basic Pokémon.

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves, using the cutters on its arms and sticky silk.
Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.
Leavanny dress it in clothes they made for it when it hatched. It hides its head in its hood while it is sleeping.
